name: Marketplace Agentic Memory Benchmark
methodology: Public-evidence architecture benchmark. Scores are 0-5 by criterion, adjusted by source confidence. This does not claim black-box latency or accuracy for closed products without API access.

criteria.label: Memory model depth and typed tiers

providers.name: MemRoOS current
providers.shortAssessment: Strongest governance-plus-workflow shape, but public benchmark proof and hot-path retrieval need to catch up.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: Has seeded recall evals with recall, precision, MRR, tier coverage, false-positive rate, and latency, but no external LOCOMO/DMR/LongMemEval-style benchmark yet.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Operator auth, per-agent write paths, audit-like episodic rows, dispatch governance, and security/planning surfaces are central to the product.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Vector, graph, episodic, qmd, knowledge, and skill surfaces create a deeper operating memory model than pure conversation stores.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: A2A, REST shim, MCP, LangGraph delegation, HIL, skills, and registry make memory part of agent operations rather than a detached API.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: Self-hosted/private-network-first posture is strong; enterprise compliance packaging remains immature.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: Has caching/performance work, but current eval path is not marketed around sub-10ms memory reads or GPU/cache economics.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: Eval engine, SEAL loop, policy lab, ledger, flow, and dashboard concepts are a clear differentiator.

portability_open_exits.rationale: Source-controlled, framework-agnostic interfaces and local-first posture give strong exits.

providers.name: MemRoOS competitive target
providers.shortAssessment: Winning target if hot memory, temporal invalidation, and public benchmark harness ship without weakening governance.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: Target adds external memory benchmarks, failure fixtures, and public reports to the existing eval substrate.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Target keeps governed memory as the primary wedge and adds policy-backed memory promotion/retention.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Target adds temporal invalidation, hot summary/context blocks, and entity/version reconciliation on top of existing tiers.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: Target keeps A2A/REST/MCP/LangGraph/HIL and makes retrieved context packs observable per run.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: Target adds explicit tenant isolation, export, retention, and compliance checklist docs.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: Target introduces hot context cache, async consolidation, tier fan-out, reranking, and benchmark latency budgets.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: Target ties every retrieval to eval traces, drift checks, and operator-approved memory self-edits.

portability_open_exits.rationale: Target keeps source-controlled, self-hostable, framework-neutral interfaces.

providers.name: AgenticMemory.ai
providers.shortAssessment: Likely the closest small closed competitor from the X signal: fast, simple, API-first, but less proven for governed enterprise workflows.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: Claims semantic search and auto summaries, but public benchmark methodology was not found.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Public page claims tenant-isolated spaces, scoped API keys, TTL, and no training on customer data.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Messages, threads, context, state, entities, entries, scratchpad, shared world memory, semantic search, and webhooks are documented primitives.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: REST, MCP, OpenClaw, LangChain, CrewAI, OpenAI Agents, AutoGen, Cursor, Semantic Kernel, Bedrock, and Vercel AI integrations are advertised.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: Hosted service with enterprise plan; no clear self-hosted/private-network deployment evidence found.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: Claims sub-millisecond hot-cache reads; not independently measured here.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: Usage analytics and memory events are suggested, but no eval/self-improvement control plane is visible.

portability_open_exits.rationale: REST/MCP lowers integration lock-in, but persistence is in a closed hosted service.

providers.name: WorldFlow AI
providers.shortAssessment: Performance-first memory layer; stronger threat to latency/cost narrative than to governed agentic memory.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: Public evidence emphasizes caching and acceleration more than recall accuracy benchmarks.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Claims edge PII detection, GDPR compliance, and SOC 2 planned.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Four pillars cover gateway cache, KV-cache acceleration, long-term memory, and cost optimization.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: Works with any LLM provider by base URL changes, but orchestration/HIL/multi-agent governance is not the main surface.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: Enterprise demo positioning is clear; deployment/control model is not deeply public.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: Claims sub-10ms cache hits, 2-12x TTFT speedup, and 40-70% cost reduction.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: AI observability and cost analytics are advertised.

portability_open_exits.rationale: Zero-code base URL swap helps adoption, but the platform appears closed.

providers.name: Tytan TAO / Cortex
providers.shortAssessment: Most enterprise-sounding governed memory competitor, but public technical proof is thin.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: No public memory benchmark methodology found.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Public page claims SOC 2 Type II, HMAC-notarized memory, RBAC/ABAC, HIL, and complete audit trails.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Cortex is described as persistent, governed, notarized memory across six layers.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: Public page claims durable DAG workflows, model routing, agent types, tool registry, and MCP enterprise adapters.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: Enterprise posture is strong, but self-host/export details were not clear from public docs.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: No clear public latency or cost benchmark found.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: Executive intelligence, audit trails, and decision explainability are central claims.

portability_open_exits.rationale: Closed agentic OS positioning implies lock-in risk unless proven otherwise.

providers.name: AXME
providers.shortAssessment: Strong orchestration/governance competitor; memory appears narrower and coding-agent focused.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: AI coding memory is claimed, but public memory quality benchmarks were not found.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Fleet governance, quarantine, policy guardrails, audit trail, and multi-tenancy are explicit platform claims.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Persistent coding memory is visible, but broader typed enterprise memory tiers are not the main claim.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: Durable execution, scheduling, time-travel replay, fleet observability, and open protocol are the core product.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: Public comparison claims self-host or hosted deployment and full portability.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: Durable execution and replay reduce wasted work, but memory latency proof is not public.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: Fleet-wide traces, logs, metrics, replay, quarantine, and policy visibility are strong.

portability_open_exits.rationale: Open protocol and self-host/hosted positioning are explicit.

providers.name: EverMind / EverMemOS
providers.shortAssessment: Important benchmark-oriented memory competitor; needs direct verification beyond press claims.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: Press release claims SOTA accuracy across four major memory benchmarks.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Enterprise-grade security is claimed, but audit/permission mechanics were not clear in public evidence.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Episodic trace formation, semantic consolidation, and reconstructive recollection are explicit architectural primitives.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: Memory platform focus is clear; multi-agent workflow/governance integration is less visible.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: Cloud API and open-source system are claimed, but enterprise deployment specifics need verification.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: Claims higher accuracy with a fraction of full-context compute cost.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: Self-organizing memory is core, but operator eval/audit workflow details are unclear.

portability_open_exits.rationale: Open-source EverMemOS improves exit posture, assuming license and implementation are acceptable.

providers.name: Mem0 Platform
providers.shortAssessment: Strong production memory layer and benchmark baseline; less differentiated on workflow governance.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: Public docs and paper describe production memory and benchmark evaluation; graph/entity behavior has changed in OSS v3.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Managed platform claims audit logs and workspace governance by default.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Platform has graph memory, multimodal, categories, filters, async, rerankers; OSS v3 removed explicit graph store support in favor of entity linking.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: Connectors and framework integrations are broad, including MCP, LangChain, CrewAI, and Vercel AI SDK.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: Managed and open-source paths exist; managed platform is the enterprise path.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: Production-scale managed service and new algorithm focus on fewer LLM calls and hybrid retrieval.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: Dashboard and activity management are present; full operator-approved self-improvement loop is not the core product.

portability_open_exits.rationale: OSS path helps, but platform features and graph behavior differ from self-hosted OSS.

providers.name: Zep
providers.shortAssessment: Probably the strongest pure memory-quality competitor because temporal invalidation is product-native.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: Temporal KG architecture and DMR benchmark claims are public.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Context engineering primitives are strong; enterprise governance posture is less central than memory quality.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Knowledge graph, facts, entities, episodes, summaries, observations, user summaries, custom edge/entity types, and fact invalidation are explicit.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: Agentic tool use and graph/context APIs are strong, but full orchestration is not the primary product.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: Commercial platform exists; self-host/export posture needs deeper review.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: Context block assembly is optimized, but public latency/cost claims are less aggressive than cache-first competitors.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: Temporal graph facts are inspectable; eval/self-improvement workflow is not the main surface.

portability_open_exits.rationale: API abstraction helps, but KG semantics may be sticky.

providers.name: Letta
providers.shortAssessment: Deep agent memory and learning architecture; closer to stateful agents than an enterprise memory control plane.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: Letta has memory-first architecture and public eval/leaderboard surfaces.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Persistent state, runs/steps, tools, and RBAC docs exist, but governed enterprise memory is not the main wedge.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Core memory blocks, shared blocks, archival memory, recall memory, messages, tools, context hierarchy, and self-editing memory are central.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: Stateful agent runs/steps, tools, MCP, HIL, multi-agent patterns, and APIs are present.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: Docker server and API platform exist; full enterprise data-control posture still needs verification.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: Focus is context management quality more than latency/cache economics.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: Runs/steps, eval docs, continual learning, background memory agents, and memory palace support strong observability.

portability_open_exits.rationale: BYO API keys, local/server options, and memory portability across models are public claims.

providers.name: GBrain
providers.shortAssessment: Relevant to the X links, but not really a closed enterprise competitor yet.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: Public discussion includes high recall claims, but independent/reproducible benchmark framing is disputed.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Personal memory/knowledge brain positioning, not an enterprise governance layer.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Persistent searchable knowledge, entity detection, enrichment, and compiled truth are reported.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: Strong fit for OpenClaw/Hermes community and agent-native workflows.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: MIT/open-source posture is strong for control, but enterprise compliance is not the focus.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: Scalability concerns for enterprise-scale deployments are noted in public analysis.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: Not enough public evidence of governed eval/observability loops.

portability_open_exits.rationale: Open-source, OpenClaw/Hermes oriented setup gives strong portability.
